steps.title: Track the competing findings
steps.content: The passage compares different findings about macroalgal mats and MPB. Choudhury and colleagues found that macroalgae do not impact MPB. Ortega and Morales found that benthic chlorophyll concentrations, a measure of MPB biomass, decreased when macroalgae were present. Mercer and Price began with a negative-effect hypothesis, but their survey found no significant correlation between benthic chlorophyll concentrations and macroalgal mat abundance.

steps.title: Reject unsupported conclusions
steps.content:
• Choice B says the finding was consistent with Ortega and Morales, but Ortega and Morales found a decrease in benthic chlorophyll with macroalgae, while Mercer and Price found no significant correlation.
  • Choice C claims study-design differences likely explain the discrepancy, but the passage gives no evidence about such differences.
  • Choice D says macroalgal mats may positively affect chlorophyll production, but a no-correlation result does not support a positive effect.

steps.title: Choose the supported comparison
steps.content: The only supported conclusion is that Mercer and Price's negative-effect hypothesis was not supported by their results. Since their no-correlation finding points away from a measurable impact of macroalgae on MPB biomass, it also fits the earlier finding by Choudhury and colleagues that macroalgae do not impact MPB. Choice A states both parts without adding an unsupported explanation or reversing the comparison with Ortega and Morales. The answer is A.
